  PHASE 2 — BATCH: skipped (singleton was a higher-confidence path for the remaining substantive item).

  PHASE 3 — SINGLETON SUBSTANTIVE (1 item):
    ATT-2026-04-22T10-33-23-429Z (WIN-010) — RESOLVED via mode2b-singleton verification. The original concern was 'FSF ratio argument is fundamentally wrong (ratio-only, not absolute magnitude).' I verified the current data/wins.json text explicitly threads the ratio-vs-magnitude distinction in finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and tldr_verdict — finding text reads 'derives the station-to-station FSF = H(r)/r ratio from its own geometry (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999); what it does not derive is the absolute magnitude at the Boulder anchor, which is imported from the 2017 observation and reused as the 2026 baseline — observation-anchored, not dome-derived.' Downstream pipeline closed the loop: ISS-1390/1391/1392 closed 2026-04-22, EXP-220 integrated 2026-04-22, EXP-244 cluster patch added Chapman 1919 / Yamazaki & Maute 2017 / Kim & Chang 2018 (page-corrected), WIN-010.c10 (reviewed 2026-04-25) verdict_holds=true with conf=0.85 and 0 outstanding_holes. No new EXP needed.

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side of the argument too'):
  Counter-argument considered: Item 1's reason text ('critical FSF factual error', 'Analyst should prioritize this') is unusually strong, and arguably could be read as a structural high-priority signal even though the OBE rule's exclusion (priority:high / blocker / critical TAG) applies to fields not text content. To respect that signal, I escalated Item 1 from OBE to singleton substantive review and verified the substantive concern is captured in current wins.json text. The other 4 items had identical structural footprint (decider-pushed FYI of patches, all referenced ISSes closed ≥3d ago, all referenced EXPs integrated, subsequent verdict-confirming curmudgeon reviews) and went OBE.

  Counter-counter on the OBE pattern: a defender could argue OBE risks dismissing a stale-but-still-needed re-analysis. Mitigations in place: every OBE has explicit one-line rationale; integrity agent can spot-check OBE rates; high-priority items excluded; the substantive Item 1 escalated. The pattern of decider creating attention items at patch time, which the downstream curmudgeon cycle then absorbs, is exactly what the OBE rule was designed for.

WORK PERFORMED (Mode 1 single-EXP run):
  Wrote monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-267.json — single tight EXP for SEC-4.2.2.c2 H1+H2 follow-up patches per c2 reviewer's recommendation.

  H1 (MAJOR — 'no physical mechanism' misattribution):
    Verified live on 2026-04-27: curl of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/predictions.html confirms Tier 1 magnetic block (line 113 of fetched HTML) reads verbatim:
      "Globe prediction: 0.0 nT systematic (or ionospheric Sq disruption — competing mechanism exists, see WIN-068 honest caveat)."
    Confirmed (via grep over fetched HTML) that 'no physical mechanism' does not appear in dome material at all. The 'no mechanism' / 'cannot produce' phrasings on the dome's site apply to gravity Tier 2 ('ionospheric mechanism cannot produce ground-level g changes') and Tier 3 ('no mechanism'), NOT magnetic Tier 1.
    EXP-267 paragraph 5 replaced: removed misattribution, quoted dome's Tier 1 verbatim, pivoted to footnote-vs-operational-rule structural critique. Preserved 'refined for over ninety years' beat by folding into new paragraph.

  H2 (MODERATE — Sq-residual fourth horn):
    Verified live on 2026-04-27: curl of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/live.html confirms Domain 11 falsification rule reads byte-identical to c2 capture: "Eclipse 2026 — Fails if measured anomaly is within ±3 nT of 0 (globe prediction)."
    EXP-267 added new paragraph after closing trilemma engaging post-Sq-residual defense: dome owes pre-registered Sq-subtraction methodology anchored before measurement (otherwise moveable goalpost); on the only reading the dome has actually anchored (raw measurement vs 0 nT), the BOU-2017 trilemma still fires.

  MINOR / polish (c2 4(a)):
    Hoisted §4.2.7 forward-link parenthetical to its own dedicated paragraph at the end of the section, so trilemma → fourth-horn → §4.2.7 link each own a closing beat. Resolves c2's rhetorical-dilution concern.

  TLDR (c2 minor 'sits outside' phrasing):
    LEFT UNCHANGED. c2 explicitly marked optional and not blocking. Body's reconstructed closing argument is more robust against post-Sq-residual escape (the readier defender line) than against TLDR-category-error. Editing TLDR risks introducing fresh micro-error for marginal gain.

  c1 stronger_arguments[0] (E001-E009 self-falsification on dome's own forecast):
    NOT incorporated (was missed-strengthening, not a hole). Decider can spawn a follow-up ISS for one-sentence add if appetite remains; not blocking.

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ('Think about the other side too') — captured in EXP-267 anticipated_objections and user_preference_directive_capture:
  Defender retreat (a): 'WIN-068 honest_caveat IS the methodology' → countered: footnote acknowledgement ≠ pre-registered methodology (no specified Sq baseline / window / station-set / aggregation).
  Defender retreat (b): 'rule applies to residual' → countered by the new fourth-horn paragraph (no published methodology, moveable goalpost).
  Defender retreat (c): 'we'll publish a Sq-subtraction methodology now' → countered: any post-hoc methodology must be OTS-anchored before August 12, 2026 to be pre-registered with respect to the measurement; if so anchored, that's the canary fix and our review updates to engage the published methodology on its merits; if unanchored, moveable-goalpost critique fires harder.
  None of these defender retreats survives without a methodological commitment that, made properly, would itself constitute the canary-detected fix.
